title: phebalium megaphyllum
intro: Phebalium megaphyllum, also known as the large-leaved phebalium, is a shrub native to Australia, belonging to the Rutaceae family. It has been traditionally used by Indigenous Australians for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to provide various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating skin conditions. In herbal preparations, the leaves and bark of Phebalium megaphyllum can be used to make teas, infusions, and topical applications, although further research is needed to fully understand its effects and potential interactions.
